Table A.13: INIT Data File Structure

A.  1.  1.  ### INIT Data -- Static Data Header Keywords

The [INIT](#__RefHeading___Toc45789_719036256) Data -- Static Data Header Keyword section is the first data set that should be read or written to the [INIT](#__RefHeading___Toc45789_719036256) Data file. This data set includes INTEHEAD, LOGIHEAD, and DOUBHEAD keywords that define versus parameters used in subsequent keywords in the [INIT](#__RefHeading___Toc45789_719036256) Data file. This is then followed by a series of global grid PROPERTY data keywords that define the various global grid property arrays for each grid cell in the model, as well as the static tabular data (relative permeability tables and PVT tables), etc.

The structure for this set of keywords is defined in .

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+--------+----+
|                                                                                                                                                                             | > INTEHEAD                                      | > Global static header keyword for the [INIT](#__RefHeading___Toc45789_719036256) Data file that defines the start of [INIT](#__RefHeading___Toc45789_719036256) static data set, and contains the integer variables for this header keyword. NIHEAD is the number of entries associated with this keyword, and is dependent on the features being used in the model. | > Required                                          |        |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+--------+----+
| > 1-1                                                                                                                                                                       | > Format                                        | > INTEHEAD                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | > NIHEAD                                            | > INTE |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+--------+----+
| > 2-1                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                 | > Undefined.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | > 0                                                 |        |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+--------+----+
| > 2-2                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                 | > Undefined.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | > 0                                                 |        |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+--------+----+
| > 2-3                                                                                                                                                                       | > Data                                          | > Unit system used in the simulation, set to 1 for metric, 2 for field, and 3 for laboratory.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |                                                     |        |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+--------+----+
| > 2-4                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                 | > Undefined.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | > 0                                                 |        |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                     |        |    |
| > to                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                     |        |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                     |        |    |
| > 2-8                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                     |        |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+--------+----+

A.  1.  1.  ### INIT Data -- Static Grid Array Data Keywords

This series of keywords define various static global and [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650) grid property arrays for each grid cell in the model. The grid array keywords use the same grid property keywords described in the [GRID](#__RefHeading___Toc38674_784232322) section of the manual. For example, the [DEPTH](#__RefHeading___Toc58139_3701168388), [PORO](#__RefHeading___Toc45797_719036256), [PERMX](#__RefHeading___Toc45791_719036256), [PERMY](#__RefHeading___Toc45793_719036256), [PERMZ](#__RefHeading___Toc45795_719036256), [NTG](#__RefHeading___Toc33334_784232322), [TOPS](#__RefHeading___Toc55283_3701168388) arrays etc. In addition, the keywords also include the calculated pore volumes ([PORV](#__RefHeading___Toc96547_718313858)) and transmissibility arrays ([TRANX](#__RefHeading___Toc93085_718313858), [TRANY](#__RefHeading___Toc93087_718313858), and [TRANZ](#__RefHeading___Toc93089_718313858)) after processing the [GRID](#__RefHeading___Toc38674_784232322) and [EDIT](#__RefHeading___Toc40641_784232322) sections. Note the first property should always be the [PORV](#__RefHeading___Toc96547_718313858) array for both global and [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650) grid types.

This format is also used to define the global grid REGION, [NNC](#__RefHeading___Toc63285_718313858), and ENPOINTS section arrays, with the keyword name corresponding to the array name. For example, for the REGION keywords the same keywords outlined in the REGION section of the manual; that is [EQLNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc73734_2752266063), [FIPNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc77229_2752266063), [PVTNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc68366_2752266063), [ROCKNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc118210_2939291539), and [SATNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc71136_2752266063) are utilized as the keywords in this section.

The structure for this type of data and the keywords are defined in .

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | > PROPERTY                                  | > The PROPERTY keyword defines the start of a grid property array section of the [INIT](#__RefHeading___Toc45789_719036256) file. PROPERTY is the keyword name of the array being written out, as outlined in the [GRID](#__RefHeading___Toc38674_784232322) section. For example, [DEPTH](#__RefHeading___Toc58139_3701168388), [PORO](#__RefHeading___Toc45797_719036256), [PERMX](#__RefHeading___Toc45791_719036256), [PERMY](#__RefHeading___Toc45793_719036256), [PERMZ](#__RefHeading___Toc45795_719036256), [NTG](#__RefHeading___Toc33334_784232322), [TOPS](#__RefHeading___Toc55283_3701168388) keywords and the calculated [PORV](#__RefHeading___Toc96547_718313858), [TRANX](#__RefHeading___Toc93085_718313858), [TRANY](#__RefHeading___Toc93087_718313858), and [TRANZ](#__RefHeading___Toc93089_718313858) arrays. | > Required |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | > The PROPERTY keyword (items 1-1 and 2-1) is repeated for each grid property.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | > The [PORV](#__RefHeading___Toc96547_718313858) property must be the first data set for this keyword type and is written out in full for each cell in the model (that is NXYZ = NX x NY x NZ values). Note the [PORV](#__RefHeading___Toc96547_718313858) array values for inactive cells are set to a constant value of zero.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | > Subsequent grid property arrays can be in any order and only the active cells are written out (NACTIV).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > 1-1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | > Format                                    | > PROPERTY                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | > NXYZ     | > REAL         |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | > or       |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | > NACTIV   |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > 2-1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | > Data                                      | > PROPERTY data set.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650) Property Data for Each [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650) Keyword and Data                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > Table and Constant Keywords ([TABDIMS](#__RefHeading___Toc89327_327352552), TAB, and CON)                                                                                                                                                         |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | > REGION                                    | > The REGION keyword defines the start of the grid region array section. REGION is the keyword name of the region array being written out, as outlined in the [REGIONS](#__RefHeading___Toc40648_784232322) section. For example, [EQLNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc73734_2752266063), [FIPNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc77229_2752266063), [PVTNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc68366_2752266063), [ROCKNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc118210_2939291539), [SATNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc71136_2752266063), etc.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | > Required |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | > For analytical aquifers in the model, the AQUIFERA variable name is used to indicate which cells are connected to a given aquifer. If a cell is connected to an aquifer number N, then the cell value will be set to 2(N-1) in the array.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | > The REGION keyword (items 1-1 and 2-1) is repeated for each global region array and the arrays can be written out in any order.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > 1-1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | > Format                                    | > REGION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | > NACTIV   | > INTE         |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > 2-1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | > Data                                      | > REGION data set.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650) Region Keywords and Data for Each Grid                                                                                                                                                                 |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | > ENDPOINT                                  | > The ENDPOINT keywords define the start of a grid\'s saturation and end-point data arrays. ENDPOINT is the keyword name of the saturation ([SWL](#__RefHeading___Toc22881_7842323221), [SWATINIT](#__RefHeading___Toc323952_1728001293), etc.) array or end-point array ([PCW](#__RefHeading___Toc84164_621662414), [KRORW](#__RefHeading___Toc70191_335817223), etc.) being written out, as described in the [GRID](#__RefHeading___Toc38674_784232322) and [PROPS](#__RefHeading___Toc39329_784232322) sections.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | > Required |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | > The type of data for the keyword should be set to REAL for arrays with real values ([SWL](#__RefHeading___Toc22881_7842323221), [SWATINIT](#__RefHeading___Toc323952_1728001293), etc) or INTE for integer arrays ([ENDNUM](#__RefHeading___Toc123125_83452205), etc). ENDPOINT arrays can be in any order and only the active cells are written out (NACTIV).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | > This keyword (items 1-1 and 2-1) is repeated for each saturation and end-point array being written out. The data written out is dependent on the various options declared in the [RUNSPEC](#__RefHeading___Toc55591_1778172979) section as well as the data declared in the [GRID](#__RefHeading___Toc38674_784232322) and [PROPS](#__RefHeading___Toc39329_784232322) sections.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > 1-1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | > Keyword                                   | > ENDPOINTS                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | > NACTIV   | > INTE or REAL |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > 2-1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | > Data                                      | > ENDPOINT data set.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
| > [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650) Saturation End-Point Keywords and Data for Each [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650)                                                                                                             |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------+----------------+----+
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | > [NNC](#__RefHeading___Toc63285_718313858) | > The [NNC](#__RefHeading___Toc63285_718313858) keyword defines the start of a grid\'s non-neighbor connection array data. [NNC](#__RefHeading___Toc63285_718313858) is the keyword name of the non-neighbor connection array being written out, and should be set to one of the following keywords:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | > Required |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | 1)  TRANNNC: the non-neighbor connection transmissibilities within the grid. Always required even if there are no [NNC](#__RefHeading___Toc63285_718313858), in which case NUMNCC is set to zero.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | 2)  DIFFNNC: the non-neighbor connection diffusivities within the grid. Only required if the Diffusivity option has been activated by the [DIFFUSE](#__RefHeading___Toc349951_1539708736) keyword in the [RUNSPEC](#__RefHeading___Toc55591_1778172979) section. This option is currently not supported by OPM Flow                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | 3)  HEATNNC: the non-neighbor connection thermal transmissibilities within the grid. Only required if OPM Flow's [THERMAL](#__RefHeading___Toc137276_650382403) option has been activated by the [THERMAL](#__RefHeading___Toc137276_650382403) keyword in the [RUNSPEC](#__RefHeading___Toc55591_1778172979) section.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | > The following two additional [NNC](#__RefHeading___Toc63285_718313858) keywords are required for when the data for an [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650) is being defined:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | 1)  TRANCC: the non-neighbor connection transmissibilities within the [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650) (always required for an [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650)).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |                |    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                             | 2)  [TRANGL](#__RefHeading___Toc1306682_4250154414): the non-neighbor connections transmissibilities between the [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650) and the global grid (always required for an [LGR](#__RefHeading___Toc55049_4106839650)).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |            |                |    |
